title: गणेश हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गणेश हृदयम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीगणेशहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य।
गणक ऋषिः। निचृद्गायत्री छन्दः।
श्रीमहागणपतिर्देवता। गणपतिप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

गणानां त्वां गणपतिं हवामहे कविं कवीनामुपमश्रवस्तमम्।
ज्येष्ठराजं ब्रह्मणां ब्रह्मणस्पत आ नः शृण्वन्नूतिभिः सीद सादनम्॥ १॥

एकदन्तं चतुर्हस्तं पाशमंकुशधारिणम्।
रदं च वरदं हस्तैर्बिभ्राणं मूषकध्वजम्॥ २॥

रक्तं लम्बोदरं शूर्पकर्णकं रक्तवाससम्।
रक्तगन्धानुलिप्ताङ्गं रक्तपुष्पैः सुपूजितम्॥ ३॥

भक्तानुकम्पिनं देवं जगत्कारणमच्युतम्।
आविर्भूतं च सृष्ट्यादौ प्रकृतेः पुरुषात्परम्॥ ४॥

गणेशहृदयं स्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वविघ्नविनिर्मुक्तो गणेशकृपामाप्नुयात्॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्री गणेश हृदयम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
benefits: Sacred Ganesha Hridayam for devotion and spiritual growth
Invokes blessings of Ganesha in all forms
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and bestows spiritual merit
Ideal for Ganesha festivals and daily worship
description: The Ganesha Hridayam, known in Sanskrit as गणेश हृदयम्, is a heart-essence prayer in praise of Lord Ganesha. This sacred composition is sourced from Purana Tradition and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke blessings of Ganesha in all forms as well as grant divine protection and auspiciousness. Additionally, it is known to remove sins and bestow spiritual merit. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi. With 5 verses and a reading time of about 3 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ganesha Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ganesha Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for Ganesha devotion and blessings.

Ganesha mool mantra Om Gam Ganapataye Namah
Ganesh Chaturthi puja vidhi at home
Sankashti Chaturthi vrat for obstacle removal
Heart hridayam stotras of Hindu deities

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganesha Hridayam and how does it differ from the Ganapati Atharvashirsha?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Hridayam is a short five-verse hymn opening with the famous Rigvedic mantra Gananam tva ganapatim havamahe — the same opening as the Ganapati Atharvashirsha — and is preserved in the puranic stotra tradition with the Rishi attribution Ganaka and the chhandas Nichrid-Gayatri. While the Ganapati Atharvashirsha (Ganapati Upanishad) is a longer Vedantic Upanishad text running approximately twenty stanzas with deep philosophical declarations of Ganesha as Brahman, the Hridayam is a more compact heart-essence devotional that emphasises the iconography (Ekadanta, Chaturhasta, Pasha-Ankusha, Mushaka-dhvaja) and red-hued worship form of Mahaganapati. (Sources: traditional stotra anthologies; Vedadhara Ganesha texts.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the term Hridayam used in stotra titles like Ganesha Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Hridayam literally means heart in Sanskrit and refers to a specific genre of stotras that present the heart-essence (sarata or sara-tattva) of a deity's worship in compact form. Famous examples include Aditya Hridayam (taught by Agastya to Rama in the Ramayana), Narayana Hridayam, Lakshmi Hridayam and Devi Hridayam. These hridaya texts typically combine a brief dhyana-shloka, a few core verses on the deity's nature and form, and sometimes a nyasa or kavacha element, making them ideal for daily personal recitation. The Ganesha Hridayam similarly distils the essential worship form of Mahaganapati into five verses. (Sources: traditional stotra classification; Vaikhanasa and Pancharatra Agama traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the iconography described in the Ganesha Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The second through fourth verses describe Ganesha as Ekadanta (single-tusked), Chatur-hasta (four-handed) holding the pasha (noose) and ankusha (elephant goad) while showing the radana (broken tusk) and varada-mudra (boon-granting gesture), riding the Mushaka-dhvaja (mouse banner/vehicle). He is described as raktam (red-hued), Lambodaram (pot-bellied), Shurpa-karnakam (winnowing-fan eared), wearing red garments (rakta-vasasam), anointed with red sandal paste (rakta-gandha), and worshipped with red flowers (rakta-pushpaih). This is the classical iconography of the Mahaganapati or Heramba Ganapati form rather than the ordinary white Vinayaka. (Sources: Mudgala Purana; Ganesha Purana iconography chapters.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ganesha Hridayam be recited for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Wednesday (Budhvar) is the day traditionally associated with Ganesha worship in many regional traditions, while Tuesday (Mangalvar) is preferred in Maharashtra. Sankashti Chaturthi (the fourth day of the dark fortnight every month) is the principal monthly observance, and Vinayaki Chaturthi (the fourth day of the bright fortnight) is the secondary one. The annual high festival is Ganesh Chaturthi on the Shukla Chaturthi of Bhadrapada month, when ten-day public Ganpati pandals are celebrated especially in Maharashtra. The Hridayam is also recited at the start of any new venture, examination, journey, business or sacred ceremony. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ganesh Chaturthi; Mudgala Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the phalashruti of the Ganesha Hridayam promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing fifth verse states that one who recites this Ganesha Hridayam with bhakti-samyutah (united with devotion) becomes free from all obstacles (sarva-vighna-vinirmukto) and obtains the grace of Ganesha (Ganesha-kripam-apnuyat). Traditional commentaries extend these benefits to success in studies and examinations, removal of vighnas in business and career, harmony in family and marriage, protection during travel, and the fulfilment of righteous wishes. As with all Ganesha stotras, it is recited at the beginning of any new undertaking to ensure obstacle-free completion (nirvighna-samaapti). (Sources: traditional Ganesha commentaries; Mudgala Purana phalashruti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Ganesha Hridayam be recited daily by householders without initiation?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the Ganesha Hridayam belongs to the category of saumya (auspicious and gentle) stotras that are open to all devotees regardless of age, gender, caste or initiation status. Unlike tantric Ganapati upasanas such as the Uchchhishta Ganapati or some Heramba Ganapati sadhanas which traditionally require diksha, the Hridayam is a devotional stuti meant for daily recitation. The simple practice is to bathe, light a lamp before a Ganesha image or picture, offer durva grass, modaka or a fruit, and recite the five verses with attention. It can also be recited mentally before any new task. (Sources: traditional stotra usage; Ganesha Purana householder practice.)
